data.headline: Bathroom Remodeling in New Haven, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut bathroom remodeling contractor serving New Haven and New Haven County , complete renovations from walk-in showers to full master suites.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in New Haven, CT
data.content:
New Haven's diverse housing includes Victorians near the Green, multi-family homes in East Rock and Westville, condos downtown, and historic residences throughout. Bathroom conditions vary dramatically , clawfoot tubs in well-maintained Victorians, worn fixtures in multi-family conversions, and builder-grade materials in newer condos.

BuiltWell provides bathroom remodeling across all New Haven neighborhoods and property types. We serve both owner-occupied homes and investment properties with materials and approaches appropriate to each.

data.title: What Is Included in a Bathroom Remodel in New Haven
data.content:
A bathroom remodel in New Haven covers demolition, waterproofing, tile, plumbing fixtures, vanity, electrical, ventilation, and all required Connecticut permits, coordinated from demo day through final walkthrough. New Haven's Victorian three-family homes have bathrooms that were added to the structure long after the original construction, typically on an upper floor where the plumbing runs down through the building in a cast-iron waste stack shared between units.

We begin with full demolition of existing tile, fixtures, vanity, and flooring, and assess the subfloor framing and all visible plumbing before materials are selected. Cast-iron waste stacks in New Haven three-families frequently show cracking or separation at horizontal branch connections, and we inspect those junctions while walls are open rather than tiling over a drain assembly that may fail once the bathroom is back in use. Subfloor framing in post-construction bathroom additions sometimes used undersized joists, and we sister as needed before tile goes in.

Waterproofing is installed behind all shower and tub areas using membrane systems, with cement board throughout wet zones. In multi-unit buildings, a waterproofing failure in an upper-floor bathroom creates damage that travels through the structure to the unit below, so the membrane installation in a New Haven triple-decker bathroom gets the same attention we would give a primary bath in a single-family home.

Finish work includes floor and wall tile, vanity and countertop installation, all plumbing fixture connections, electrical updates for GFCI compliance, and exhaust fan installation. In New Haven condo and rental conversions, compact wall-hung vanities are often the right choice because they preserve floor area in bathrooms that were never large to begin with. Permit coordination and a final walkthrough are included.

data.title: What Makes Bathroom Remodeling in New Haven Different
data.content:
New Haven's architectural diversity means bathroom renovations require sensitivity to each property's character. Victorian homes near the Green may have original clawfoot tubs and pedestal sinks worth preserving or incorporating into a modern design. East Rock colonials have standard mid-century bathrooms. Downtown condos require building management coordination.

Multi-family properties have specific requirements , durable materials, waterproofing between units, and finishes that balance quality with practical longevity.

data.title: New Haven Building Department and Permits
data.content: The New Haven Building Department requires permits for bathroom renovations involving plumbing, electrical, or structural work. Multi-family properties may have additional requirements. Historic district properties near the Green may have review requirements. We identify all requirements during planning.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling Cost in New Haven, CT
data.subtitle: Bathroom remodeling costs in New Haven depend on the scope of work, material selections, and the condition of existing plumbing and structure. Ranges are illustrative for typical Connecticut projects. Final pricing depends on scope, materials, site conditions, and permit fees. Detailed written estimate provided before any work begins.

data.title: Why Bathroom Remodeling in New Haven Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
New Haven homes present bathroom conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

New Haven homes built before 1920 , common in East Rock, Westville, and near the Green , may have original plumbing systems that present unique renovation conditions: lead supply lines, terra cotta drains, and plaster walls with irregular framing that require careful assessment.

Multi-family bathroom renovations require waterproofing that prevents leaks between units. We use enhanced membrane systems and conduct pan-testing before tile installation in all multi-family bathroom projects.

data.title: Our Bathroom Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every bathroom remodel follows the same five-step process , structured to keep projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.description: We visit your home, assess the existing bathroom conditions , plumbing, electrical, ventilation, structural , and discuss your goals. No charge. No obligation. We identify potential issues upfront so there are no surprises during construction.

steps.title: Planning
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering every trade: tile, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, vanity, fixtures, glass, and paint. Each line item is broken out so you understand exactly where the budget is going. We include a complete project schedule.

steps.title: Selections
steps.description: We guide you through tile, vanity, fixture, and hardware selections with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so everything arrives before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. Demolition, rough plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity installation, fixture connections, and paint , all sequenced properly. You receive daily updates and a clean, dust-controlled workspace.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished bathroom together , every tile, every grout line, every fixture, every piece of hardware. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks from demolition to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1–2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Material Lead Time
items.description: 1–4 Weeks. Vanity, tile, fixtures, and glass enclosure ordering and delivery.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 2–4 Weeks. Demo, plumbing, electrical, waterproofing, tile, vanity, fixtures, and paint.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 2–3 Days. Hardware, accessories, glass installation, cleanup, and walkthrough.

data.title: Bathroom Remodeling in New Haven , Questions

items.question: How long does a bathroom remodel take in New Haven?
items.answer: Most New Haven bathroom remodels take three to eight weeks of active construction, plus material lead time. A hall bath typically takes 3-4 weeks. A full master bath remodel takes 5-8 weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: How much does bathroom remodeling cost in New Haven?
items.answer: Bathroom remodeling in New Haven ranges from $8,000 for a half-bath update to $100,000+ for a upscale master suite with upgraded stone, radiant heat, and custom features. A standard hall bath remodel typically falls between $15,000 and $30,000. A master bath with walk-in shower and double vanity runs $30,000 to $60,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade.

items.question: Do I need a permit for bathroom remodeling in New Haven?
items.answer: Yes. Most bathroom remodeling projects in New Haven require permits from the New Haven Building Department, particularly for plumbing and electrical work. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project.

items.question: What types of bathrooms do you remodel in New Haven?
items.answer: We handle all bathroom types in New Haven: half baths, hall baths, master baths, guest baths, and basement bathrooms. New Haven's housing stock includes Victorians, multi-family homes, condos, and historic residences. We're familiar with the construction conditions in these homes and account for them in every proposal.

items.question: Can you convert my tub to a walk-in shower in New Haven?
items.answer: Yes. Tub-to-shower conversions are one of our most requested bathroom renovations in New Haven. The project typically involves removing the existing tub, modifying the drain location, installing waterproofing membrane, and building a tiled walk-in shower with glass enclosure. Most conversions take 2-3 weeks and cost between $12,000 and $25,000 depending on tile selections and shower configuration.
